name: KyzoScript
comment: Hand-written from crates/kyzo-core/src/kyzoscript.pest (story #73's editor-tooling scaffold), not a generated artifact -- every pattern below cites the grammar rule it renders. Re-derive against the grammar when the two drift; a stale highlighter is the same defect class as a stale doc.

patterns.comment: BLOCK_COMMENT = _{ "/*" ~ (BLOCK_COMMENT | !"*/" ~ ANY)* ~ "*/" } -- nestable, so the pattern includes itself.
patterns.name: comment.block.kyzoscript

patterns.comment: raw_string: a fence of zero-or-more `_` then `".."`, closed by `"` plus the SAME number of `_` (PUSH/POP in the grammar). No escapes at all inside.
patterns.name: string.quoted.raw.kyzoscript

patterns.comment: quoted_string: double-quoted, with the escapes s_char/quoted_string_inner accept.
patterns.name: string.quoted.double.kyzoscript

patterns.comment: s_quoted_string: single-quoted, the same escapes as double-quoted.
patterns.name: string.quoted.single.kyzoscript

patterns.comment: s_char: \' \" \\ \/ \b \f \n \r \t, or \u followed by 4 hex digits.
patterns.name: constant.character.escape.kyzoscript

patterns.comment: hex_pos_int / octo_pos_int / bin_pos_int, `_` digit-group separators allowed.
patterns.name: constant.numeric.integer.kyzoscript

patterns.comment: dot_float: digits, a decimal point, digits, optional exponent. Ordered before the bare-integer rule below so `1.5` isn't read as `1` then punctuation then `5`.
patterns.name: constant.numeric.float.kyzoscript

patterns.comment: sci_float: digits with an exponent and no decimal point (`1e10`).
patterns.name: constant.numeric.float.kyzoscript

entry-marker.comment: prog_entry: the `?` that names a script's one output rule.
entry-marker.name: keyword.control.entry.kyzoscript

patterns.comment: param: `$name`, substituted from the parameter pool at parse time.
patterns.name: variable.parameter.kyzoscript

patterns.comment: relation_ident / *rel forms: `*` naming a stored relation in a body atom, distinct from the `*` multiplication operator by always being followed immediately by an identifier.
patterns.name: entity.name.type.relation.kyzoscript

patterns.comment: search_apply head: `~relation:index{...}` -- a similarity/full-text search atom.
patterns.name: entity.name.type.search.kyzoscript

patterns.comment: compound_or_index_ident with no sigil: the bare `relation:index` shape `::hnsw`/`::fts`/`::lsh create` take (index_create_adv). Matched before #directives/#identifiers so `doc:emb` reads as one qualified name, not `doc` plus a stray `:emb` directive.
patterns.name: entity.name.type.relation.kyzoscript

directives.comment: Every `:option`/`:relation-op` and every `::sys-op` shares one prefix shape: `:` or `::` then a bare identifier. One general pattern instead of an enumerated keyword list, so a sys-op or option kyzoscript.pest adds later still highlights -- an enumerated list would silently miss it and go stale.
directives.name: keyword.control.directive.kyzoscript

imperative-keywords.comment: The `%`-imperative block-control vocabulary (imperative_stmt/imperative_sysop in the grammar): `%if`, `%then`, `%else`, `%end`, `%loop`, `%break`, `%continue`, `%return`, `%swap`, `%debug`, `%mark`, `%ignore_error`, `%if_not`, and any future `%word` the grammar adds.
imperative-keywords.name: keyword.control.imperative.kyzoscript

word-operators.comment: or_op / in_op / not_op: word-shaped operators, boundary-checked so `origin` doesn't light up as `or`+`igin`.
word-operators.name: keyword.operator.word.kyzoscript

validity-clause.comment: `@` opens a validity clause on either side (read: `rel{x @ instant}`; write: `:put rel {...} @ instant`), optionally `@spans`/`@delta`/`@delta_sys`.
validity-clause.name: keyword.operator.validity.kyzoscript

types.comment: col_type keywords from the schema grammar (bool_type/int_type/.../vec_type element names), for `{col: Type}` schema clauses.
types.name: storage.type.kyzoscript

identifiers.comment: ident / var / compound_ident: everything else that starts a word -- rule names, column names, variables.
identifiers.name: variable.other.kyzoscript
